steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a country that uses "dinar" in its currency name. A correct answer must (1) name a specific country and (2) show that country's currency includes the word "dinar."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Latin word "denarius" is the origin of the Arabic word "dinar.": This is about etymology, not about any country's currency. No country is named. ✗
  • Choice B — Today, several countries use the word "dinar" in naming their currencies.: Mentions countries in general but names none. An example requires a specific country. ✗
  • Choice C — Bahrain uses the Bahraini dinar as its national currency.: Names a specific country (Bahrain) and explicitly shows its currency contains the word "dinar." This is exactly the example the student needs. ✓
  • Choice D — Bahrain does not refer to its currency by the Latin word "denarius.": Names Bahrain but focuses on what its currency is not called. It does not demonstrate that Bahrain uses "dinar" in its currency name. ✗
That matches Choice C — Bahrain uses the Bahraini dinar as its national currency..
